title: Vincetoxicum dorgelonis
taxonomy: Vincetoxicum dorgelonis is a species of flowering plant in the Asclepiadaceae family, which is now commonly referred to as Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Vincetoxicum and is native to China. The exact classification of Vincetoxicum dorgelonis may be subject to change as taxonomic research continues. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure. Further study is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of this species.

intro: Vincetoxicum dorgelonis, also known as Dorgelon's Vincetoxicum, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, fever, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Vincetoxicum dorgelonis include vincamine alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used to make extracts and dried preparations, such as capsules and tablets, to support overall health and well-being.
